code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of food‑related triples into a single fluent sentence.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Build subject to predicates mapping
    subject_data = {}
    for t in triples:
        if t.subject not in subject_data:
            subject_data[t.subject] = []
        subject_data[t.subject].append((t.predicate, t.object))

    # Find main subject (food item - typically has most food-related predicates)
    food_predicates = {"ingredient", "mainIngredient", "dishVariation", "course",
                       "servingTemperature", "served", "carbohydrate", "fat", "protein",
                       "year", "related", "similarDish", "alternativeName", "creator",
                       "creatorOfDish", "servingSize", "division", "family", "order",
                       "genus", "class"}

    main_subject = None
    max_food_preds = 0

    for subject in subject_data:
        food_count = sum(1 for p, o in subject_data[subject] if p in food_predicates)
        if food_count > max_food_preds:
            max_food_preds = food_count
            main_subject = subject

    if not main_subject:
        main_subject = list(subject_data.keys())[0]

    # Build predicate phrase mapping
    phrases = {
        "country": "from {obj}",
        "region": "originating in {obj}",
        "ingredient": "containing {obj}",
        "mainIngredient": "with main ingredients {obj}",
        "dishVariation": "variations include {obj}",
        "course": "served as a {obj}",
        "servingTemperature": "served {obj}",
        "served": "served {obj}",
        "carbohydrate": "with {obj} carbohydrates",
        "fat": "with {obj} fat",
        "protein": "with {obj} protein",
        "year": "introduced in {obj}",
        "related": "related to {obj}",
        "similarDish": "similar to {obj}",
        "alternativeName": "also known as {obj}",
        "creator": "created by {obj}",
        "creatorOfDish": "created by {obj}",
        "servingSize": "with serving size {obj}",
        "division": "division {obj}",
        "family": "family {obj}",
        "order": "order {obj}",
        "genus": "genus {obj}",
        "capital": "capital is {obj}",
        "isPartOf": "part of {obj}",
        "foundationPlace": "found in {obj}",
        "leader": "led by {obj}",
        "language": "language {obj}",
        "officialLanguage": "official language {obj}",
        "currency": "currency {obj}",
        "demonym": "demonym {obj}",
        "ethnicGroup": "ethnic group {obj}",
        "leaderTitle": "leader title {obj}",
        "class": "class {obj}"
    }

    def get_predicate_phrase(p, o):
        template = phrases.get(p, "{p} {obj}")
        return template.replace("{p}", p).replace("{obj}", o)

    # Build all clauses starting from main subject
    visited = set()
    all_clauses = []

    def process_subject(subject, prefix=""):
        if subject in visited:
            return
        visited.add(subject)

        if subject in subject_data:
            clauses = []
            for p, o in subject_data[subject]:
                if p in ["country", "region", "isPartOf"]:
                    clauses.append(f"which is {get_predicate_phrase(p, o)}")
                else:
                    clauses.append(f"{get_predicate_phrase(p, o)}")

            if clauses:
                all_clauses.append((subject, clauses))

    process_subject(main_subject)

    # Build final sentence
    if not all_clauses:
        return main_subject + "."

    parts = []
    for subject, clauses in all_clauses:
        if len(clauses) == 1:
            parts.append(f"{subject} {clauses[0]}")
        else:
            all_but_last = ", ".join(clauses[:-1])
            parts.append(f"{subject} {all_but_last}, and {clauses[-1]}")

    if len(parts) == 1:
        sentence = parts[0]
    else:
        sentence = parts[0]
        for part in parts[1:]:
            sentence += f", {part}"

    return sentence + "."

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END

from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of food‑related triples into a single fluent sentence.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Build graph of relationships
    graph = {}
    reverse_graph = {}  # object -> (subject, predicate)
    for t in triples:
        s, p, o = t.subject, t.predicate, t.object
        if s not in graph:
            graph[s] = []
        graph[s].append((p, o))
        if o not in reverse_graph:
            reverse_graph[o] = []
        reverse_graph[o].append((s, p))

    # Find the main subject (food item - typically has most food-related predicates)
    food_predicates = {"ingredient", "mainIngredient", "dishVariation", "course",
                       "servingTemperature", "served", "carbohydrate", "fat", "protein",
                       "year", "related", "similarDish", "alternativeName", "creator",
                       "creatorOfDish", "servingSize"}

    main_subject = None
    max_food_preds = 0

    for subject in graph:
        food_count = sum(1 for p, o in graph[subject] if p in food_predicates)
        if food_count > max_food_preds:
            max_food_preds = food_count
            main_subject = subject

    if not main_subject:
        main_subject = list(graph.keys())[0]

    # Build predicate phrase mapping
    phrases = {
        "country": f"from {{obj}}",
        "region": f"originating in {{obj}}",
        "ingredient": f"containing {{obj}}",
        "mainIngredient": f"with main ingredients {{obj}}",
        "dishVariation": f"variations include {{obj}}",
        "course": f"as a {{obj}}",
        "servingTemperature": f"served {{obj}}",
        "served": f"served {{obj}}",
        "carbohydrate": f"with {{obj}} carbohydrates",
        "fat": f"with {{obj}} fat",
        "protein": f"with {{obj}} protein",
        "year": f"introduced in {{obj}}",
        "related": f"related to {{obj}}",
        "similarDish": f"similar to {{obj}}",
        "alternativeName": f"also known as {{obj}}",
        "creator": f"created by {{obj}}",
        "creatorOfDish": f"created by {{obj}}",
        "servingSize": f"with serving size {{obj}}",
        "division": f"division {{obj}}",
        "family": f"family {{obj}}",
        "order": f"order {{obj}}",
        "genus": f"genus {{obj}}",
        "capital": f"capital is {{obj}}",
        "isPartOf": f"part of {{obj}}",
        "foundationPlace": f"found in {{obj}}",
        "leader": f"led by {{obj}}",
        "language": f"language {{obj}}",
        "officialLanguage": f"official language {{obj}}",
        "currency": f"currency {{obj}}",
        "demonym": f"demonym {{obj}}",
        "ethnicGroup": f"ethnic group {{obj}}",
        "leaderTitle": f"leader title {{obj}}",
        "class": f"class {{obj}}"
    }

    def get_predicate_phrase(p, o):
        template = phrases.get(p, "{{p}} {{obj}}")
        return template.replace("{p}", p).replace("{obj}", o)

    # Build all clauses starting from main subject
    visited = set()
    all_clauses = []

    def process_subject(subject, prefix=""):
        if subject in visited:
            return
        visited.add(subject)

        if subject in graph:
            clauses = []
            for p, o in graph[subject]:
                if p in ["country", "region", "isPartOf"]:
                    clauses.append(f"which is {get_predicate_phrase(p, o)}")
                else:
                    clauses.append(f"{get_predicate_phrase(p, o)}")

            if clauses:
                all_clauses.append((subject, clauses))

            # Process connected subjects through reverse graph
            if subject in reverse_graph:
                for obj, pred in reverse_graph[subject]:
                    if obj not in visited:
                        process_subject(obj, f"where the {pred} is {subject}")

    process_subject(main_subject)

    # Build final sentence
    if not all_clauses:
        return main_subject + "."

    parts = []
    for subject, clauses in all_clauses:
        if len(clauses) == 1:
            parts.append(f"{subject} {clauses[0]}")

            parts.append(f"{subject} {all_but_last}, and {clauses[-1]}")

    if len(parts) == 1:
        sentence = parts[0]

            sentence += f", {part}"

    return sentence + "."

from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of food‑related triples into a single fluent sentence.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Build subject to predicates mapping
    subject_data = {}
    for t in triples:
        if t.subject not in subject_data:
            subject_data[t.subject] = []
        subject_data[t.subject].append((t.predicate, t.object))

    # Find main subject (food item - typically has most food-related predicates)
    food_predicates = {"ingredient", "mainIngredient", "dishVariation", "course",
                       "servingTemperature", "served", "carbohydrate", "fat", "protein",
                       "year", "related", "similarDish", "alternativeName", "creator",
                       "creatorOfDish", "servingSize", "division", "family", "order",
                       "genus", "class"}

    main_subject = None
    max_food_preds = 0

    for subject in subject_data:
        food_count = sum(1 for p, o in subject_data[subject] if p in food_predicates)
        if food_count > max_food_preds:
            max_food_preds = food_count
            main_subject = subject

    if not main_subject:
        main_subject = list(subject_data.keys())[0]

    # Build predicate phrase mapping
    phrases = {
        "country": "from {obj}",
        "region": "originating in {obj}",
        "ingredient": "containing {obj}",
        "mainIngredient": "with main ingredients {obj}",
        "dishVariation": "variations include {obj}",
        "course": "served as a {obj}",
        "servingTemperature": "served {obj}",
        "served": "served {obj}",
        "carbohydrate": "with {obj} carbohydrates",
        "fat": "with {obj} fat",
        "protein": "with {obj} protein",
        "year": "introduced in {obj}",
        "related": "related to {obj}",
        "similarDish": "similar to {obj}",
        "alternativeName": "also known as {obj}",
        "creator": "created by {obj}",
        "creatorOfDish": "created by {obj}",
        "servingSize": "with serving size {obj}",
        "division": "division {obj}",
        "family": "family {obj}",
        "order": "order {obj}",
        "genus": "genus {obj}",
        "capital": "capital is {obj}",
        "isPartOf": "part of {obj}",
        "foundationPlace": "found in {obj}",
        "leader": "led by {obj}",
        "language": "language {obj}",
        "officialLanguage": "official language {obj}",
        "currency": "currency {obj}",
        "demonym": "demonym {obj}",
        "ethnicGroup": "ethnic group {obj}",
        "leaderTitle": "leader title {obj}",
        "class": "class {obj}"
    }

    def get_predicate_phrase(p, o):
        template = phrases.get(p, "{p} {obj}")
        return template.replace("{p}", p).replace("{obj}", o)

    # Build all clauses starting from main subject
    visited = set()
    all_clauses = []

    def process_subject(subject, prefix=""):
        if subject in visited:
            return
        visited.add(subject)

        if subject in subject_data:
            clauses = []
            for p, o in subject_data[subject]:
                if p in ["country", "region", "isPartOf"]:
                    clauses.append(f"which is {get_predicate_phrase(p, o)}")
                else:
                    clauses.append(f"{get_predicate_phrase(p, o)}")

            if clauses:
                all_clauses.append((subject, clauses))

    process_subject(main_subject)

    # Build final sentence
    if not all_clauses:
        return main_subject + "."

    parts = []
    for subject, clauses in all_clauses:
        if len(clauses) == 1:
            parts.append(f"{subject} {clauses[0]}")
        else:
            all_but_last = ", ".join(clauses[:-1])
            parts.append(f"{subject} {all_but_last}, and {clauses[-1]}")

    if len(parts) == 1:
        sentence = parts[0]
    else:
        sentence = parts[0]
        for part in parts[1:]:
            sentence += f", {part}"

    return sentence + "."
